Submission Metadata (Oman HTA)

GDP confirmation, COI disclosure, and public dossier availability are required for submission readiness.

Model Structure

Oman HTA Guidance

Jump to Embedded Library

Official Guidance Summary

Based on the Ministry of Health Oman Guidelines for Health Technology Assessment (2024) and peer-reviewed methodological guidance and roadmap publications.

Official MOH Peer-reviewed Roadmap Public Announcements

Use this section as a submission checklist. Full extracted text is in the Guidance Library.

Methodological Sections

  • Target indication
  • Medical assessment
  • Economic evaluation
  • Budget impact analysis
  • Ethical, organizational, and patient/social aspects
  • Transparency of HTA dossiers and updates

Comparators and Health Gain

  • Use a policy-relevant comparator aligned with clinical guidance.
  • Health benefit evaluation should prioritize mortality, morbidity, and quality of life.
  • Clinical assessment reports from reputable HTA agencies are encouraged.

Economic Evaluation

  • Cost-utility with QALYs is preferred when added value is demonstrated.
  • Cost-minimization is only for equivalent outcomes.
  • Health care (payer/provider) perspective is the required base case.
  • Discount costs and outcomes at 3%.
  • Apply explicit cost-effectiveness thresholds based on GDP per capita.

Budget Impact Analysis

  • 4-year projection horizon.
  • No discounting in budget impact.
  • Deterministic sensitivity analysis is mandatory (±10%).

Transparency

  • Prepare both full and public versions of the HTA dossier.
  • Declare conflicts of interest and expert contributions.
  • Review and update guidelines and checklists every 3 years.

Implementation Roadmap

  • 1-2 years: capacity building and training programs.
  • 3-5 years: expand HTA unit, introduce cost-effectiveness thresholds.
  • 6-10 years: publish recommendations, appraisal reports, and timelines.

Key Excerpts

"The Omani HTA guidelines consist of six sections: target indication, medical assessment, economic evaluation, budget impact analysis, social and ethical considerations, and transparency."

Source: Methodological Guidelines (PMC)

"Cost-utility analysis with QALYs is preferred when added value is demonstrated; cost-minimization only for equivalent outcomes."

"Discount all future costs and outcomes at a rate of 3%."

Source: Methodological Guidelines (PMC)

"Budget impact analysis should cover a four-year period."

"Deterministic sensitivity analysis is mandatory with a 10% variation."

Source: Methodological Guidelines (PMC)

"The cost-effectiveness threshold is 1x GDP per capita, with higher thresholds for priority, rare diseases, or significant health gains."

Source: Methodological Guidelines (PMC)

"Full disclosure of conflicts of interest and public availability of HTA dossiers are mandatory."

Source: Methodological Guidelines (PMC)

"In the short term (1-2 years), efforts will focus on building capacity through training programs. For medium-term actions (3-5 years), plans include expanding the HTA unit and introducing multiple cost-effectiveness thresholds while from 6-10 years, publishing of HTA recommendations, critical appraisal reports, and timelines is recommended."

Source: PMC HTA Roadmap

Sources

  • Methodological guidelines for HTA in Oman (PMC)
  • Methodological guidelines record (PubMed)
  • ISPOR Europe 2024 session (Oman HTA guidelines)
  • Oman HTA roadmap and adoption planning (PubMed)

ISPOR pages may require login/cookie consent; embedded excerpts remain available below.

Quick Start Guide

                                
Load a real Oman health dataset and run a complete cost-effectiveness analysis in 3 steps:

                                
    
                                    
  1. Choose a template - Select from 10 real HTA datasets below
    2. 
                                    
  2. Review & customize - Check parameters in the Model section
    3. 
                                    
  3. Run analysis - Click "Run Model" for base case, PSA, DSA, and EVPI
    4.

ACS Incidence

                                    
Per 100,000 - among highest globally

Learning Through Stories

                        
Understanding HTA concepts and evidence reversals through memorable narratives

                    

                    

                        
                        

                            
Understanding HTA Concepts

                            

                                

                                    
NHS

                                    
The NHS Founding Vision

                                    
How Britain's 1948 healthcare dream led to the birth of HTA and NICE

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
1987

                                    
Oregon's Fateful List

                                    
The controversial 1987 attempt to rank 700+ treatments by cost-effectiveness

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
VIOXX

                                    
The Vioxx Disaster

                                    
How hidden uncertainty in trial data led to 27,785 heart attacks

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
AMD

                                    
The Million Dollar Question

                                    
Avastin vs Lucentis: How EVPI justified a trial that saves billions

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
HCV

                                    
The Hepatitis C Revolution

                                    
Egypt's bold gamble: From $84,000 to $300 per cure

                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        
                        

                            
Evidence Reversal Case Studies

                            
Real medical practices that were widely adopted, then proven ineffective or harmful by rigorous trials.

                            

                                

                                    
Cardiovascular

                                    
The Stent Story

                                    
When Doing More Doesn't Mean Doing Better

                                    
Coronary stents for stable angina - COURAGE & ORBITA trials showed no benefit over medication alone.

                                    
HIGH relevance to Oman - CVD is leading cause of death

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
Orthopedics

                                    
The Knee Surgery Parable

                                    
Seeing Is Not Always Believing

                                    
Knee arthroscopy for osteoarthritis - sham surgery trials showed it's no better than placebo.

                                    
MEDIUM relevance - aging population, rising obesity

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
Women's Health

                                    
The Hormone Pendulum

                                    
When "Obvious" Benefits Become Harms

                                    
HRT for cardiovascular prevention - WHI trial showed it INCREASED heart attacks and strokes.

                                    
MEDIUM relevance - growing elderly population

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
Critical Care

                                    
The Sugar Control Story

                                    
When "Normal" Becomes Dangerous

                                    
Tight glucose control in ICU - NICE-SUGAR showed it INCREASED deaths by 14%.

                                    
HIGH relevance - high diabetes prevalence

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
Cardiovascular

                                    
The Heartbeat Paradox

                                    
When Fixing the Rhythm Stops the Heart

                                    
Antiarrhythmic drugs post-MI - CAST trial: drugs increased deaths 2.5x despite "working" on ECG.

                                    
HIGH relevance - high ACS incidence (338.9/100,000)

                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        
                        

                            
Key Lessons for HTA

                            

                                

                                    
1

                                    

                                        Biological plausibility is not proof
                                        
Just because something SHOULD work doesn't mean it DOES work.

                                    

                                

                                

                                    
2

                                    

                                        Surrogate endpoints can deceive
                                        
Improving a test result doesn't always improve patient outcomes.

                                    

                                

                                

                                    
3

                                    

                                        Observational data has hidden biases
                                        
People who CHOOSE treatments differ from those who don't.

                                    

                                

                                

                                    
4

                                    

                                        First positive trials are often wrong
                                        
Replication in large, multicenter trials is essential.

                                    

                                

                                

                                    
5

                                    

                                        Old habits die hard
                                        
Even disproven treatments persist for years after evidence emerges.

Three-Level Meta-Analysis

                        

                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
About Three-Level Meta-Analysis

                            

                                Handles dependent effect sizes from multiple outcomes per study.
                                Decomposes heterogeneity into within-study (Level 2) and between-study (Level 3) variance components.
                                Reference: Cheung (2014), Van den Noortgate et al. (2013).
                            

                        


                        

                            
Settings

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            

                                
                                
-

                                
-

                            


                            

                                

                                    
tau-sq (Between-Study)

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
tau-sq (Within-Study)

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
I-sq Level 2

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
I-sq Level 3

                                    
-

                                

                            


                            
Interpretation

                            

                                

                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
Multivariate Meta-Analysis

                        

                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
About Multivariate Meta-Analysis

                            

                                Jointly analyzes multiple correlated outcomes (e.g., efficacy and safety) while accounting for within-study and between-study correlations.
                                More efficient than separate univariate analyses when outcomes are correlated.
                            

                        


                        

                            
Outcomes

                            

                                

                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                

                            

                            
                        


                        

                            
Pooled Effects by Outcome

                            

                                
                                    

                                        Outcome
                                        Estimate
                                        95% CI
                                        z-value
                                        p-value
                                    

                                
                                
                            


                            
Between-Study Correlation Matrix

                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
Dose-Response Meta-Analysis

                        

                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
About Dose-Response Meta-Analysis

                            

                                Models non-linear relationships between dose/exposure and outcome using restricted cubic splines.
                                Reference: Orsini et al. (2012), Crippa & Orsini (2016).
                            

                        


                        

                            
Settings

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            

                                

                                    
Non-Linearity Test

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
p-value

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
Overall Association

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
tau-sq

                                    
-

                                

                            


                            
Dose-Response Curve

                            

                                
                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
Component Network Meta-Analysis

                        

                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
About Component NMA

                            

                                Decomposes complex interventions into individual components to estimate the contribution of each.
                                Useful for behavioral interventions, multimodal treatments, and combination therapies.
                                Reference: Welton et al. (2009), Rucker et al. (2020).
                            

                        


                        

                            
Settings

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
Component Effects

                            

                                
                                    

                                        Component
                                        Effect
                                        95% CI
                                        p-value
                                        Contribution
                                    

                                
                                
                            


                            
Predict Combination Effect

                            

                                

                                
                                

                                    Predicted Effect: -
                                    -
                                

                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
Living Systematic Review

                        

                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
About Living Systematic Reviews

                            

                                Sequential meta-analysis with alpha-spending functions for continuous updating.
                                Controls Type I error while allowing interim analyses as new evidence accumulates.
                                Reference: Simmonds et al. (2017), Elliott et al. (2017).
                            

                        


                        

                            
Monitoring Settings

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            

                                

                                    

                                        
                                        
-

-

                                

                                

                                    
Z-Statistic

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
Critical Value

                                    
-

                                

                                

                                    
Information Fraction

                                    
-

                                        Date
                                        Studies

                                        Z-stat
                                        Critical
                                        Decision

Risk of Bias Assessment

                        

                            
                            
                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
ROB-2 (RCTs)

                            
ROBINS-I (Observational)

                            
Traffic Light Plot

                        


                        
                        

                            

                                
ROB-2: Risk of Bias in Randomized Trials

                                

                                    Cochrane ROB-2 tool for assessing risk of bias in randomized controlled trials.
                                    Assesses 5 domains: randomization, deviations, missing data, measurement, selection.
                                

                            


                            
Domain Assessments

                            

                                
                                    

                                        Domain
                                        Judgment
                                        Supporting Information
                                    

                                
                                
                                    

                                        D1: Randomization process
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D2: Deviations from interventions
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D3: Missing outcome data
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D4: Measurement of outcome
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D5: Selection of reported result
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                
                            


                            

                                
Overall ROB-2 Judgment

                                
-

                            

                        


                        
                        

                            

                                
ROBINS-I: Risk of Bias in Non-Randomized Studies

                                

                                    Tool for assessing risk of bias in non-randomized studies of interventions.
                                    Assesses 7 domains including confounding, selection, and classification biases.
                                

                            


                            

                                
                                    

                                        Domain
                                        Judgment
                                        Supporting Information
                                    

                                
                                
                                    

                                        D1: Confounding
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D2: Selection of participants
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D3: Classification of interventions
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D4: Deviations from intended interventions
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D5: Missing data
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D6: Measurement of outcomes
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                    

                                        D7: Selection of reported result
                                        
                                            
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                
                            


                            

                                
Overall ROBINS-I Judgment

                                
-

                            

                        


                        
                        

                            

                                
Risk of Bias Summary (Traffic Light Plot)

                                

                                    Visual summary of risk of bias assessments across all included studies.
                                    Generated using robvis-style visualization.
                                

                            

                            

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
PRISMA 2020 Flow Diagram

IPD Meta-Analysis (Individual Patient Data)

                        

                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
Analysis Type

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
IPD Data Input

                            
Paste patient-level data (CSV format with columns: study, patient, treatment, outcome, [covariates])

                            
                        


                        

                            
IPD Analysis Results

                            

                                
Run analysis to see results...

                            

                        


                        

                            
About IPD Meta-Analysis

                            
Individual Patient Data meta-analysis is the gold standard for evidence synthesis. It enables:

                            
    
                                
  • Treatment-covariate interactions: Identify which patients benefit most
    • 
                                
  • Subgroup analyses: More reliable than aggregate data subgroups
    • 
                                
  • Standardized outcomes: Consistent definitions across studies
    • 
                                
  • Missing data handling: Multiple imputation at patient level
    • 
                            

                            
Reference: Debray TPA et al. (2015). Get real in individual patient data (IPD) meta-analysis. Res Synth Methods.

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
DTA Meta-Analysis (Diagnostic Test Accuracy)

Data Fabrication Detection (GRIM/SPRITE/GRIMMER)

                        

                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
Test Selection

                            

                                

                                    
                                    

                                        
                                        
                                        
                                        
                                    

                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
Study Data

                            
Enter summary statistics (study, n, mean, sd)

                            
                        


                        

                            
Results

                            

                                

                                    
                                        

                                            Study
                                            GRIM
                                            GRIMMER
                                            Status
                                        

                                    
                                    
                                        
Run tests to see results...

                                    
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
Interpretation Guide

                            
    
                                
  • GRIM: Tests if reported means are mathematically possible given sample size
    • 
                                
  • GRIMMER: Extends GRIM to standard deviations
    • 
                                
  • SPRITE: Attempts to reconstruct raw data from summary statistics
    • 
                                
  • StatCheck: Verifies consistency of reported statistics (t, F, p)
    • 
                            

                            
Note: Failures may indicate errors, not necessarily fraud. Always investigate in context.

                        

                    

                


                
                

                    

                        
ML-Assisted Screening & Extraction

Federated Meta-Analysis (Privacy-Preserving)

                        

                            
                        

                    

                    

                        

                            
Privacy Settings

                            

                                

                                    
                                    
                                

                                

                                    
                                    
                                    Lower = more private, less accurate
                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
Site Summary Statistics

                            
Each site shares only summary statistics, not individual data

                            
                        


                        

                            
Results

                            

                                

                                    

                                        
Pooled Estimate

                                        
-

                                        
95% CI: -

                                    

                                    

                                        
Privacy Guarantee

                                        
-

                                        
-

                                    

                                

                            

                        


                        

                            
Site Contributions

                            

                                
                            

                        


                        

                            
About Federated Meta-Analysis

                            
Enables collaborative research without sharing raw patient data:

                            
    
                                
  • GDPR/HIPAA compliant: Only aggregate statistics leave each site
    • 
                                
  • Differential privacy: Adds calibrated noise to prevent re-identification
    • 
                                
  • Multi-site collaboration: Pool evidence across institutions securely
    • 
                            

                        

                    

                


            

        

    


    
    

        

            

            
Loading...

        

    


    
    


    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    

    

    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    

    
    
    

    
    
    

    
    

    
    

    
    

    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    
    
    
    
    
    

    
    
    

    
    
    

    
    
    

    
    

    
    

    
    

    

    
    
    
    
    

    
    

    
    

        

            

                Keyboard Shortcuts
                
            

            

                
Navigation

                

                    Show keyboard shortcuts
                    ?
                

                

                    Close modal / Cancel
                    Esc
                

                

                    Go to Summary
                    G then S
                

                

                    Go to Parameters
                    G then P
                

                

                    Go to Results
                    G then R
                

            

            

                
Actions

                

                    Run base case analysis
                    Ctrl + Enter
                

                

                    Run PSA
                    Ctrl + Shift + P
                

                

                    Save / Export JSON
                    Ctrl + S
                

                

                    Undo
                    Ctrl + Z
                

                

                    Redo
                    Ctrl + Y
                

            

            

                
Interface

                

                    Toggle dark mode
                    Ctrl + D
                

                

                    Toggle language (AR/EN)
                    Ctrl + L
                

                

                    Toggle sidebar
                    Ctrl + B
